repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
fixes https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39071
close https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37284

`api.nvim_buf_del_mark` already emits a `MarkSet` event with `col` and `line` set to 0. However, `:delmarks` currently emits no events. This changes `:delmarks` to emit the same `col==line==0` event, and adds a test asserting the existing behavior of `api.nvim_buf_del_mark`.

---

There's several ways of implementing this and I wasn't sure of the best "style", e.g. some questions I have:

- instead of manually calling `do_markset_autocmd`, we could change `ex_delmarks` to use `setmark_pos`
  - this has a slight change of behavior with how the `:` mark is handled (`setmark_pos` allows deleting it if `bt_prompt` is true)
- instead of manually enumerating marks in `:delmarks!`, we could make `clrallmarks` trigger the event
  - possibly by adding a bool parameter, since `clrallmarks` is called when a buffer is being deleted, and presumably we don't want to emit an event then
- possibly only trigger a `MarkSet` event if the mark previously existed

feel free to give feedback, and ask me to change to some other way of implementing this! (this is my first PR to neovim)

comment_data.body:
would you mind adding a hint to the `clear_fmark` and `clrallmarks` docstrings:

    /// Does not trigger Markset event.
~~seems a bit noisy to fire N events for the "clear all" case, maybe we should special-case this as `ev.name = 'ALL'` ? especially since the line/col are useless.~~

hmm, yeah... this is partially why I was thinking "only trigger events for marks that previously existed", since then this wouldn't trigger events for the whole alphabet if they didn't already exist.

as a user, I think I would slightly prefer N events - the exact behavior of `:delmarks!` is really unintuitive IMO (it does not clear uppercase or numerical marks), and it's easy as a plugin author to misinterpret `ev.name == 'ALL'` as, well, "all", so explicitly listing which marks actually got deleted is nice, instead of needing to know and reimplement the wonky behavior of `:delmarks!` in plugin code.

but totally reasonable to just have an "all" event, let me know if I didn't convince you and you'd prefer an `ev.name = 'ALL'` event :P
oh, more unintuitive behavior, `:delmarks!` also doesn't clear the `'<` and `'>` marks :skull:
agree, let's go with N events 😓

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -204,6 +201,8 @@ These existing features changed their behavior.
   • |nvim_create_autocmd()|
   • |nvim_exec_autocmds()|
   • |nvim_get_autocmds()|
+• |:delmarks| now triggers the |MarkSet| autocommand with line==col==0, same
+  as |nvim_buf_del_mark()|

comment_data.body:
> if it's an upper case mark, to send the event on the buffer the mark is set on, instead of the current buffer

is that covered here? if not would be good to do so (same `it()` case)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -283,4 +283,57 @@ describe('MarkSet', function()
     eq({ 1, 1 }, api.nvim_buf_get_mark(third_bufnr, 'B'))
     eq({ 3, 0 }, api.nvim_buf_get_mark(first_bufnr, 'C'))
   end)
+
+  it('emits when marks are deleted', function()

comment_data.body: I put this into a different test (in the same file) that already had multi-buffer testing set up, hope that's ok!
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -283,4 +283,57 @@ describe('MarkSet', function()
     eq({ 1, 1 }, api.nvim_buf_get_mark(third_bufnr, 'B'))
     eq({ 3, 0 }, api.nvim_buf_get_mark(first_bufnr, 'C'))
   end)
+
+  it('emits when marks are deleted', function()

comment_regular_data.body: btw https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37284 had some checks for `curbuf->b_last_cursor.mark.lnum != 0` , do we need those here?

> btw #37284 had some checks for `curbuf->b_last_cursor.mark.lnum != 0` , do we need those here?

Ah, yeah, that's the check for:

> > possibly only trigger a `MarkSet` event if the mark previously existed
> 
> that sounds like a good idea

(`lnum == 0` means the mark doesn't exist)
pushed an update - also stole a change from #37284 which is, if it's an upper case mark, to send the event on the buffer the mark is set on, instead of the current buffer
Superbly done! Nit for future reference, it is very helpful if the commit message + PR description has problem/solution format. I fixed the commit message in this case.

Backport failed for `release-0.12`, because it was unable to cherry-pick the commit(s).

Please cherry-pick the changes locally and resolve any conflicts.
```bash
git fetch origin release-0.12
git worktree add -d .worktree/backport-39156-to-release-0.12 origin/release-0.12
cd .worktree/backport-39156-to-release-0.12
git switch --create backport-39156-to-release-0.12
git cherry-pick -x 4b54bca3dfb0d06f4c5298565839994ec2e8b650
```
